agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on updated Amenity Cost Charges program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on an updated Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) program and related bylaw amendments. If approved, this would change the fees developers pay to help fund community amenities like parks, community centres, and libraries when new development happens. The proposed bylaw would set new charge rates and rules for collecting these fees. This is not yet approved — council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, these charges will affect development costs for new construction projects in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 ACC PROGRAM & BYLAW AMENDMENT
Purpose: To request Council approval of an amended Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) Program & related amendments to the Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to urge Province to restore climate action funding
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider sending a letter to the provincial government asking them to renew funding for the Local Government Climate Action Program (LGCAP). This program helps local governments pay for projects that reduce greenhouse gas emissions. The Province recently cut this funding, and Burnaby is asking them to bring it back. This is a proposed advocacy action — no new rules or fees for businesses are being voted on yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now. Watch for the Council decision — if approved, the letter will be sent to the Province.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2 ADVOCACY FOR RENEWED PROVINCIAL LOCAL GOVERNMENT CLIMATE ACTION PROGRAM FUNDING
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the provincial Local Government Climate Action Program (LGCAP) funding cut and request Mayor and Council forward a letter to the Province to advocate for renewed funding for LGCAP.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on rental replacement rule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider minor amendments to Stream 1 of the Rental Use Zoning Policy. These changes would align the policy with recent updates to rental replacement requirements. If approved, the amendments could affect how developers and landlords handle replacing rental units when redeveloping property in Burnaby. The proposal is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — these changes could affect your rental replacement obligations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3 PROPOSED AMENDMENTS TO RENTAL USE ZONING POLICY STREAM 1 - RENTAL REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To propose minor amendments to Stream 1 of the Rental Use Zoning Policy to align with recent changes to rental replacement requirements.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to comment on Metro Vancouver growth strategy amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is considering a comment letter opposing Metro Vancouver Amendment Bylaw No. 1456, 2026, which would eliminate the prohibition on new Special Study Areas (SSAs) and allow any member jurisdiction to request new SSAs of any size and location before December 31, 2027. SSAs make it easier to re-designate Agricultural, Rural, Conservation and Recreation, and Employment lands and to expand the Urban Containment Boundary, facilitating sprawl with added costs to regional DCCs and TransLink services, environmental harm, loss of food-producing land, speculative pressure, and traffic congestion. The bylaw also adds a 152-hectare SSA in Langley Township on Fraser Highway to re-designate Rural lands to Industrial.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this comment letter — it could signal future changes to regional growth rules that affect your business.

key_dates.label: Deadline for member jurisdictions to request new Special Study Areas under proposed bylaw
Burnaby Council meeting to consider comment letter on Amendment Bylaw No. 1456

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4 COMMENT LETTER ON METRO VANCOUVER REGIONAL DISTRICT REGIONAL GROWTH STRATEGY AMENDMENT BYLAW NO. 1456, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with the opportunity to consider forwarding a comment letter to the Metro Vancouver Regional District Board in response to Amendment Bylaw No. 1456 to amend the Metro 2050 Regional Growth Strategy.

attachments.title: Comment Letter on Metro Vancouver Regional District Regional Growth Strategy Ame

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on funding for Kensington Overpass and tunnel murals
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a proposal to approve funding for new artistic murals on the Kensington Overpass pillars and the Lougheed Pedestrian Tunnel South entrance walls. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. If approved, the murals would be created on these public structures. The purpose is to beautify the area and support local art.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5 MURAL FUNDING PROPOSAL - KENSINGTON OVERPASS SOUTH AND LOUGHEED PEDESTRIAN TUNNEL SOUTH ENTRANCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for funding for the creation of artistic murals on the Kensington Overpass pillars and Lougheed Pedestrian Tunnel South entrance walls.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on paying 2026 LMLGA membership dues
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to pay the 2026 membership dues for the Lower Mainland Local Government Association (LMLGA). The LMLGA is a group that represents local governments in the Lower Mainland region. If approved, the city would pay these dues to stay a member of the association. This is a routine administrative decision that keeps Burnaby connected with other local governments in the region.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6 LMLGA 2026 MEMBERSHIP DUES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for payment of the Lower Mainland Local Government Association (LMLGA) 2026 membership dues.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to change signing officers on City bank accounts
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to change who is authorized to sign on the City's bank accounts. This is an internal administrative matter that updates the list of officials who can approve payments and financial transactions on behalf of the City. The change does not directly affect business owners, but it ensures the City's banking operations remain secure and properly managed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7 SIGNING OFFICERS - BANKING
Purpose: To change signing officers on the City bank accounts.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Douglas Road and Laurel Street road rehabilitation contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the Douglas Road and Laurel Street road rehabilitation project. This proposed decision would approve a contract for construction work to repair and upgrade these roads. The project is part of the city's capital plan and has not yet been approved. A 'contract award' means the city would officially hire a company to do the work.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8 CA - (CA-3037) DOUGLAS ROAD AND LAUREL STREET ROAD REHABILITATION
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the (CA – 3037) Douglas Road and Laurel Street Rehabilitation project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for 2026 Lougheed Highway roadworks
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the 2026 Lougheed Highway Roadworks project. This proposed decision would approve a specific company to carry out road repairs and improvements on Lougheed Highway. The work is planned for 2026, so this is an early step to lock in a contractor. If approved, the project could mean lane closures, detours, and construction noise for businesses along that stretch of highway during the construction period.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9 CA - (CA-3368) 2026 LOUGHEED HIGHWAY ROADWORKS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the (CA-3368) 2026 Lougheed Highway Roadworks project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Gilmore Pump Station upgrade and forcemain
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the Gilmore Pump Station upgrade and forcemain initiative. This proposed project would upgrade the pump station and related pipes to improve wastewater and stormwater infrastructure in the area. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the contract award at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10 CA - (EA-8038 & EA-3350) GILMORE PUMP STATION UPGRADE AND FORCEMAIN INITIATIVE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the (EA-8038 & EA-3350) Gilmore Pump Station Upgrade & Forcemain Initiative project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Infor software support contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for annual support and maintenance of Infor (Canada) software. If approved, this would authorize the city to spend money to keep this software running and up to date. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11 CA - INFOR (CANADA) SOFTWARE ANNUAL SUPPORT AND MAINTENANCE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for Infor (Canada) Software Annual Support and Maintenance.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on contract change for Victory St. watermain and road work
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the Victory Street watermain replacement and road upgrade project. If approved, this change would adjust the budget or scope of the existing construction contract. The project involves replacing aging water pipes and upgrading the road on Victory Street. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.12 CC - (EA-3233) VICTORY ST. WATERMAIN REPLACEMENT & ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EA-3233) Victory St. Watermain Replacement & Road Upgrades project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing sand supply contract for golf courses and sports fields
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract for the supply and delivery of sand used at city golf courses and sports fields. The proposed contract renewal would keep the same supplier for another term. This decision affects the maintenance of municipal recreation facilities, not private businesses directly. However, if you operate a business that relies on these facilities (e.g., a golf course concession or sports field rental), the quality and availability of sand could impact playing conditions and your operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.13 CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVER GOLF COURSE & SPORTS FIELD SAND
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Golf Course and Sports Field Sand.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Festivals Burnaby Grant Program for July 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed grant program called 'Festivals Burnaby' for July 2026. If approved, this program would provide funding to support local festivals and community events in Burnaby. The decision is scheduled for the upcoming council meeting. Business owners who run or plan festivals should watch for the council's decision, as it could affect available funding for their events.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this grant program — it could affect funding for your festival or event.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1 ECC - FESTIVALS BURNABY GRANT - JULY 2026
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Festivals Burnaby Grant Program, July recommendation.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on July 2026 Community Grant applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to approve the July 2026 Community Grant application. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. If passed, the grant would provide funding to local community groups and non-profits. Business owners who partner with or rely on these groups should watch for the council's decision, as it could affect local events, services, or partnerships.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, check if your business can apply for or benefit from the grant.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Community Grant application — council vote date

agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2 ECC - JULY 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATION
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the July 2026 Community Grant Application.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Arts Council annual operating funding of $25,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving $25,000 in annual operating funding for the Burnaby Arts Council. This funding is needed to keep the Arts Council eligible for grants from other levels of government. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3 ECC - BURNABY ARTS COUNCIL OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Arts Council's annual operating funding of $25,000, ensuring it remains eligible for funding from other levels of government.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Edmonds Community School playground grant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed grant to fund a new playground at Edmonds Community School. If approved, the grant would pay for playground equipment and installation. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. The vote will happen at an upcoming Burnaby Council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4 ECC - EDMONDS COMMUNITY SCHOOL - PLAYGROUND GRANT
Purpose: To seek Council approval for a playground grant for Edmonds Community School.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Rowing BC sport hosting grant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed Sport Hosting Grant for Rowing BC. If approved, this grant would provide funding to help Rowing BC bring a major rowing event to Burnaby. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting. This is not yet approved — it is a proposal that council will consider.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if you operate a business that could benefit from event-related tourism.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5 ECC - ROWING BC - SPORT HOSTING GRANT
Purpose: To seek Council approval for a Sport Hosting Grant for Rowing BC.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Six-storey rental building at 5168–5192 Sidley Street, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a referral to staff to gather more information on the best value proposal for the land at 5168, 5180, and 5192 Sidley Street. The current plan is for a six-storey market rental building under the Royal Oak Community Plan. However, council is also asking staff to look at a revised design that could be 12 to 20 storeys tall. This is a proposed change — nothing has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and rental supply.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.6 PDC - REFERRAL: REZ #26-07 - 5168, 5180 AND 5192 SIDLEY STREET - SIX STOREY MARKET RENTAL (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)
Purpose: To refer the report to staff to provide information on the best value proposal for the land, taking a revised design of a 12 to 20-story building into consideration.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 2025/2026 Accessibility Plan updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider an update on the city's Accessibility Plan for 2025/2026. The report covers proposed amendments to the plan, progress on implementing accessibility improvements, and a list of completed projects. This is a proposed update — Council has not yet approved the changes. If passed, the plan would guide how the city removes barriers for people with disabilities in areas like public spaces, buildings, and services. Business owners should be aware that new accessibility requirements could affect their properties or operations in the future.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, new accessibility rules may affect your business property or operations.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1 AAC - ACCESSIBILITY PLAN - IMPLEMENTATION UPDATE 2025/2026
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on Accessibility Plan amendments, implementation progress, and completed accessibility improvements in 2025/2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review RCMP policing performance report for Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a status update on RCMP policing operations covering February to March 2026, along with the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report for January to March 2026. This is a routine report that gives Council a look at how the RCMP is performing against its annual plan. No new rules or fees are proposed — this is an information item only. The report covers things like crime statistics, response times, and progress on policing priorities set for the year.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2 PSC - SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (FEBRUARY - MARCH 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (JANUARY - MARCH 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on policing operations covering February to March 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fire Department 2025 Annual Report for council review
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the Burnaby Fire Department's Annual Report for 2025 at an upcoming meeting. This report covers the department's activities, response statistics, and performance up to the end of December 2025. It is a proposed document for council's information and discussion — it has not yet been approved or adopted. The report may highlight trends or issues that could affect local businesses, such as fire response times or new safety initiatives.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3 PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT ANNUAL REPORT 2025
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's Annual Report available to the end of December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fire Department status update for April 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update from the Burnaby Fire Department covering operations up to the end of April 2026. This is a proposed report for information only — no new rules or fees are being voted on at this time. The update may highlight staffing, equipment, or response-time trends that could affect future decisions on fire safety or emergency services in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4 PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (APRIL 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of April 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw amendment — definitions and fee schedule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on an amendment to the Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw 2024. This proposed bylaw would change some definitions and update Schedule B, which lists the fees developers pay to help fund community amenities like parks, community centres, and libraries. If approved, the new rules would affect how much developers are charged when building new projects in Burnaby. The amendment is currently at the first, second, and third reading stage — meaning council is considering it but has not yet made a final decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect development costs for any new construction project in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14837 - Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw 2024 (definitions, Schedule B)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Agreement for non-market rental at 6887 Palm Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 6887 Palm Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the building as affordable rental housing for a set period. This is not yet approved — Council will give first, second, and third readings to the bylaw before a final decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2 #14841 - Burnaby Housing Agreement (6887 Palm Avenue) Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to authorize the execution of a Housing Agreement for the non-market rental housing development at 6887 Palm Avenue

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to repeal housing agreement for 5227 Lane Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would repeal a previous housing agreement for the property at 5227 Lane Street. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that sets rules about how the property can be used, often to ensure affordable housing. If approved, this repeal would cancel that existing agreement, potentially changing what the owner can do with the property. This is a final adoption vote, meaning the decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect property use rules at 5227 Lane Street.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1 #14836 - Burnaby Housing Agreement (5227 Lane Street) Bylaw 2024, Repeal Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Housing Agreement (5227 Lane Street) Bylaw 2024

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: High-density mixed-use development on Dow Avenue abandoned
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to abandon a proposed zoning bylaw amendment for 6645-6707 Dow Avenue. The amendment would have allowed a large mixed-use development with two high-rise residential towers, a nine-storey commercial podium, street-level shops, townhomes, and a six-storey rental building above underground parking. The decision to abandon means the project will not move forward as planned.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you — this proposal is being abandoned, so no further steps are required.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1 #14575 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 12, 2023 - REZ #18-49 (6645/59/75/91, 6707 Dow Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a high-density mixed-use development above underground parking. The development is comprised of two high-rise residential strata buildings, a nine-storey commercial podium, street-fronting retail, street-oriented townhomes, and a six-storey rental building

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on abandoning Silver Avenue rezoning for high-density towers
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to abandon a proposed zoning bylaw amendment for 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue. The amendment would have allowed construction of a 33-storey residential strata apartment building with street-oriented townhomes, plus a six-storey replacement rental building, all above underground parking. Council will consider dropping this proposal entirely — meaning the project would not move forward as planned.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the amendment is abandoned, this development will not proceed as proposed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.2 #14600 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 27, 2023 - REZ #21-36 (6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a high-density multiple family residential development above underground parking. The development is comprised of one 33-storey residential strata apartment building with street-oriented townhomes, and a six-storey replacement rental building
